ddls:
CREATE TABLE "energy_data" (
  "record_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each record */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the data was recorded */,
  "watts" REAL /* Power consumption in watts */,
  "volts" REAL /* Voltage in volts */,
  "amps" REAL /* Current in amps */,
  "power_factor" REAL /* Power factor */,
  "mark" TEXT /* Identifier for the data stream or sensor */,
  "data_type" TEXT /* Type of data (e.g., voltage, current, power factor) */,
  "unit" TEXT /* Unit of measurement for the data */,
  "version" TEXT /* Version of the dataset */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the data was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the data was updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("record_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sensors" (
  "sensor_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sensor */,
  "sensor_name" TEXT /* Name of the sensor */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location where the sensor is installed */,
  "type" TEXT /* Type of the sensor (e.g., power, voltage, current) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the sensor */,
  "manufacturer" TEXT /* Manufacturer of the sensor */,
  "model" TEXT /* Model number of the sensor */,
  "installation_date" TEXT /* Date when the sensor was installed */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sensor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sensor_data_mapping" (
  "mapping_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each mapping entry */,
  "record_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data record */,
  "sensor_id" INTEGER /* ID of the sensor that collected the data */,
  "collection_interval" INTEGER /* Interval at which data is collected from the sensor */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("mapping_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sensor_data_mapping_record_id FOREIGN KEY ("record_id") REFERENCES energy_data ("record_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sensor_data_mapping_sensor_id FOREIGN KEY ("sensor_id") REFERENCES sensors ("sensor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., analyst, engineer, admin) */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password for the user (hashed for security) */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last login */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* Permissions for the user (e.g., view, edit, delete) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "record_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data record being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the data */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the data was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the data was accessed */,
  "browser_agent" TEXT /* Browser agent used to access the data */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_access_logs_record_id FOREIGN KEY ("record_id") REFERENCES energy_data ("record_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_reports" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each report */,
  "title" TEXT /* Title of the report */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the report */,
  "generation_date" TEXT /* Date when the report was generated */,
  "file_path" TEXT /* File path to the report */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the report (e.g., pending, generated, failed) */,
  "report_format" TEXT /* Format of the report (e.g., PDF, CSV, Excel) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alerts" (
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert */,
  "record_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data record that triggered the alert */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the alert */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the alert was generated */,
  "severity" TEXT /* Severity level of the alert */,
  "handled" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if the alert has been handled */,
  "resolved_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who resolved the alert */,
  "resolution_notes" TEXT /* Notes about the resolution of the alert */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_resolved_by FOREIGN KEY ("resolved_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_record_id FOREIGN KEY ("record_id") REFERENCES energy_data ("record_id")
)
